title: cochlearia groenlandica subsp. islandica
intro: Cochlearia groenlandica subsp. islandica, also known as the Icelandic Rock Cress, is a subspecies of the Arctic Rock Cress plant, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, due to the presence of glucosinolates and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Icelandic Rock Cress include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
